factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the Renewable Energy and Electric Power segments.

evidence.text: Revenues increased due to a $1.73 billion increase in revenues from our Renewable Energy segment and a $520.6 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power segment, partially offset by a $225.6 million decrease in revenues from our Underground and Infrastructure segment.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2024 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2023

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand in key segments, supported by significant revenue increases.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, particularly in the Electric Power segment.

evidence.text: The increases in both remaining performance obligations and backlog from December 31, 2023 to September 30, 2024 were partially due to the impact of acquisitions that occurred in the nine months ended September 30, 2024 as well as increased new project awards with existing customers, and the increase in backlog was also attributable to new project awards included in the Electric Power segment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing backlog, particularly in Electric Power, suggests increased future demand and business activity.

factors.summary: End markets are growing fastest in Renewable Energy and Electric Power, while Underground and Infrastructure is experiencing a decline.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2024 was primarily due to approximately $450 million in revenues attributable to acquired businesses and the overall mix of work performed during the period, which included higher emergency restoration services revenues as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2023.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with growth in some areas and decline in others.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by compensation expenses and non-cash stock compensation.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily attributable to a $93.6 million increase related to recently acquired businesses; a $31.7 million increase in compensation expense, largely associated with increased salaries, incentive compensation and non-cash stock compensation expense due primarily to an increase in number of employees to support business growth and increased incentive compensation due primarily to increased levels of profitability; a $17.9 million increase in travel and related expenses to support business growth; and an $11.9 million loss on the disposal of a non-core business.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2024 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2023

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing labor costs driven by compensation and stock expenses pose a financial headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, particularly in the Renewable Energy segment.

evidence.text: Operating margin for the three months ended September 30, 2024 was favorably impacted by higher margin emergency restoration services revenues offset by lower operating margins associated with telecommunication projects.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expanding margins, particularly in Renewable Energy, indicate improved profitability.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand, particularly in the Renewable Energy and Electric Power segments.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2024 was primarily due to approximately $450 million in revenues attributable to acquired businesses and the overall mix of work performed during the period, which included higher emergency restoration services revenues as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2023.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing construction activity and infrastructure spending drive demand in key segments.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value, particularly in the Renewable Energy and Electric Power segments.

evidence.text: The increases in both remaining performance obligations and backlog from December 31, 2023 to September 30, 2024 were partially due to the impact of acquisitions that occurred in the nine months ended September 30, 2024 as well as increased new project awards with existing customers, and the increase in backlog was also attributable to new project awards included in the Electric Power segment.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions and new projects significantly contribute to backlog growth, indicating strategic value creation.
